h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner continues to seek reassurance and practice sorting the table elements by position.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Rubric gate: cover the header row. Anything hidden by the cover: header. Anything still visible in a row cell: value.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts from verbal sorting rules to a physical 'cover test' representation to address the learner's anxiety about word-pull.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Rubric gate: cover the header row. Anything hidden by the cover: header. Anything still visible in a row cell: value.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor shifts from verbal sorting rules to a physical 'cover test' representation to address the learner's anxiety about word-pull.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Rubric gate: cover the header row. Anything hidden by the cover: header. Anything still visible in a row cell: value.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor shifts from verbal sorting rules to a physical 'cover test' representation to address the learner's anxiety about word-pull.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to struggle with distinguishing between 'surface' and 'table' as separate forces.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: When the support is named as a table, the blank can say table. When the problem only says surface, surface is enough. The blank names this contact object, not a new force. One contact, one source label.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor adapts by stacking the cards physically and articulating a clear rule to resolve the learner's confusion about duplicate contact labels.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: When the support is named as a table, the blank can say table. When the problem only says surface, surface is enough. The blank names this contact object, not a new force. One contact, one source label.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor adapts by stacking the cards physically and articulating a clear rule to resolve the learner's confusion about duplicate contact labels.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: When the support is named as a table, the blank can say table. When the problem only says surface, surface is enough. The blank names this contact object, not a new force. One contact, one source label.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor adapts by stacking the cards physically and articulating a clear rule to resolve the learner's confusion about duplicate contact labels.
